content.visible_text: Distance-learning resources Kids Program Forms, ages 6–9 The following forms lessons were filmed during an online Forms Seminar  on May 16, 2020. These distance-learning resources were developed during the Shelter-in-Place ordinances of the 2020 Covid-19 pandemic. They don’t function as replacements to our in-person classes, but remain available for our students who would like to do supplemental martial arts at home. To view the written steps to each form, short videos of each form as it is to be performed, and the techniques learned at each rank, please click through the Kids Curriculum page , choosing the belt your student is * currently * wearing. View a big-picture overview of the Kids Program rank belt curriculum here . Consider downloading and printing it for visual motivation! Basic Form: White Belt Chon-Ji: Yellow Belt Dan Gun: High Yellow Belt Do San: Green Belt Gravity: High Green Belt Terra Moto: Blue Belt Kids Classes Below is a collection of one-hour-long classes that each begin with a 30 minute warmup/stretching session, followed by a 30 minute martial arts lesson. These videos are intended to support students with continued practice during times they are not able to make it to the Dojo, such as vacations or quarantine periods. The videos are recordings of live Zoom classes broadcast from the San Francisco Dojo during Spring 2020, when we were closed due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Kids Class: Footwork Kids Class: Combos Kids Class: Targets Kids Class: Kicking Kids Class: Stances Reviews Facebook Community Dojo Quantum Martial Arts is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit in the State of California, and we thrive on the generosity of people like you. All donations are tax-deductible. Quantum’s tax ID number is 26-1534597. No donation is too small, or too large. Whether it’s $20 or $20,000, every cent will be hugely appreciated and put to good use. Support Our Kids Donate now to support our youth scholarship program which allows families in need to train at reduced cost. We believe investment in our community involves investing in our youth. Our Sister Schools Our Seattle Dojo has served thousands of students since opening on Capitol Hill in 1995, and currently offers youth and adult classes in the Central District. Our Portland Dojo was established in 2020, and currently offers youth and adult classes in Southeast Portland. A WordPress.com Website .
